name: Super Minds 6 — 2nd Edition
source: Teacher's Book (620K chars) + Student's Book (172K chars)

units.title: Back to School (Starter)
units.grammar: Revision: present simple, present continuous, past simple, present perfect, comparatives.
key_phrases: I've been learning English for five years.
What did you do over the holiday?
How long have you studied English?

units.title: High-Tech World
units.topic: Technology, future with will, going to, present continuous for future
units.grammar: Will, going to, present continuous for future plans. / I'm going to study robotics. / We'll use AI more.
key_phrases: Robots will help us in the future.
I'm going to buy a drone.
The train leaves at 3pm.

units.title: Amazing Animals
units.grammar: Cheetahs are found in Africa. / Passive voice (present simple). / is/are + past participle
key_phrases: Coffee is grown in Brazil.
Penguins are found in Antarctica.
Is English spoken here?

units.title: Ancient Egypt
units.grammar: The pyramids were built thousands of years ago. / Passive voice (past simple). / was/were + past participle
key_phrases: The pyramids were built by the Egyptians.
Was this tomb discovered recently?
These artifacts were found in the desert.
units.clil: History: Daily life in Ancient Egypt

units.title: The Environment
units.grammar: If we recycle more, the planet will be cleaner. / You should save water. / You shouldn't waste energy.
key_phrases: We should turn off lights.
If the ice caps melt, sea levels will rise.
What would you do to help the planet?

units.title: At the TV Studio
units.grammar: He said (that) he was tired. / She told me (that) the show was great. / Reported speech. / You like TV, don't you? / Question tags
key_phrases: She said she loved the show.
He told me he wanted to be a reporter.
It's a great episode, isn't it?
units.clil: Media Studies: How TV programmes are made

units.title: What's It Worth?
units.grammar: This is the phone that I want. / The boy who sits next to me... / Relative clauses (who, which, that)
key_phrases: The bag that she bought was expensive.
Do you know someone who collects coins?
This is the shop which sells cheap books.

units.title: Medical Mysteries
units.topic: Health, past perfect, reported speech review
units.grammar: When the doctor arrived, the patient had already recovered. / Past perfect. / He said he had been ill.
key_phrases: He had already taken the medicine.
She said she had never felt so sick.
By the time we got to hospital, she had woken up.

units.title: On Stage
units.topic: Performing arts, wish/if only, modal verbs review
units.grammar: I wish I could sing better. / If only I had more time to practice. / Wish/If only for present wishes
key_phrases: I wish I was a famous actor.
If only we had more time!
Break a leg!

units.title: Looking Back
units.grammar: Review all tenses: present, past, present perfect, future, conditionals, passive, reported speech.
key_phrases: I've learned so much this year.
What will you remember most?
I used to be afraid of speaking English.
